# Janus

## Unified Multi-Modal AI by DeepSeek

177 followers

## Unified Multi-Modal AI by DeepSeek

177 followers

Visit website

The Janus series by DeepSeek offers powerful AI models for unified multimodal understanding and generation. It includes Janus-Pro (advanced reasoning), Janus (decoupled visual encoding), and JanusFlow (harmonized autoregression and rectified flow).

ElevenAgents by ElevenLabs

ElevenAgents by ElevenLabsScale conversations without scaling your team

Promoted

Zac Zuo

Flowtica Scribe

Flowtica Scribe

Hunter

📌 [...] Hey everyone! DeepSeek is ON FIRE! 🔥 They just dropped the Janus series – a new family of AI models focused on unified multimodal understanding and generation. Here's the breakdown: ✨ Janus-Pro: The top-tier model, trained with more data and a larger size for advanced multimodal reasoning and high-quality image generation. 🧩 Janus: Features a decoupled visual encoding architecture, offering flexibility and strong performance in vision-language tasks. ⚡ JanusFlow: Integrates rectified flow with an autoregressive model for enhanced generative capabilities. The Janus series stands out by unifying both understanding and generation across vision and language in a single framework. DeepSeek is also pushing the boundaries with novel architectures, including decoupled visual encoding and the [...] architectures, including decoupled visual encoding and the integration of rectified flow. You can download the models now and explore their capabilities!

results.title: Gemini Omni: Create anything from any input – starting with video | Product Hunt
results.content:
Geoffrey Reed

Love the focus on editing and world understanding instead of text to video generation. That’s where practical creative workflows start emerging.

Oyetunde Daniel

for the free version, will watermark be included if someone want to download the video after the prompt

Geethikareddy Konda

Interesting !! here isn't just the video generation t's the combination of world understanding and editing in one unified model. That's the hard part everyone's been quietly working on. [...] The object tracking across frames and physics-aware generation sound promising for B-roll generation too. Right now most creators use generic stock footage because creating custom visuals is too expensive. How does Omni handle consistency when you're making multiple edits to the same project — does it maintain a "memory" of previous changes or does each prompt start fresh?

Kas Fard
SANIYA JESWANI

The multimodal video-first direction feels much more practical than most AI demos lately. Combining reasoning with generation opens interesting possibilities for editing and accessibility workflows. how you maintain temporal consistency across longer video generations?

Geoffrey Reed [...] Cyrus Elmtalab

@saaswarrior  The video-first direction is interesting for launch and campaign assets. The hard part for marketing teams is usually consistency across variants: same product, same message, different channels. Curious whether Omni can preserve a campaign's style and visual rules across several generated clips or if each prompt needs to restate the constraints.

Javier Jimeno

Video editing through natural language is something every YouTube creator I talk to asks for. The gap today isn't generating video — it's editing existing footage without spending 3 hours in Premiere Pro. If I could tell an AI "cut this 20-minute video to the best 8 minutes and add captions," that alone would save creators 5+ hours per week.

Launched on August 26th, 2024 [...] #### What's great

The AI will simply ignore prompts. It seems to often decide what it wants and no matter how you change your prompts it will doggedly keep wasting your time and money and just spit out relatively the same thing over and over. There is no way anymore of giving the AI anything other than positive feedback. There is NO CUSTOMER SERVICE!!! The only human contact is through “billing” and billing CANCELS YOUR SUBSCRIPTION WITHOUT ASKING YOU IF YOU EVER COMPLAIN AND/or REQUEST A REFUND FOR TIME/MONEY their prompt-illiterate AI HAS WASTED

#### What needs improvement

They should teach their AI to actually follow prompts. They should also allow for at least some kind of camera control
